---
title: "Figure 5 (Frobenius) — correctness audit: bias + distortion (Frobenius norm) x 5 algorithms, one noise"
author: "Luciano Moffatt"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

<!--
Variant of figure_5_master with panel B measuring each distortion matrix by the FROBENIUS NORM of its
deviation from the identity, ||D - I||_F = sqrt(sum_ij (D_ij - delta_ij)^2), instead of the log-determinant.
Columns = 5 algorithms (NR, MNR, R, MR, IR). Panel A (bias) is IDENTICAL to the master: score-based bias =
inv(median Gaussian FIM) . mean-score at the truth. Panel B, 3 rows (Information, Sample, Correlation): the
Frobenius norm is a MAGNITUDE >= 0 (0 <=> self-consistent), so the scale is one-sided sequential white->red,
no sign. The eigenvalue spectrum is NOT pre-computed for the Sample/Correlation matrices, so we assemble
each 6x6 CENTRAL (mean over replicates) distortion matrix Likelihood_{Information,Sample,Correlation}_
Distortion and compute the norm from it directly. This is the "distortion of the typical matrix"; it equals
the pre-computed per-replicate-averaged scalar for the well-conditioned algorithms (IR/R/MR) and differs for
the ill-conditioned ones (NR/MNR), where the average matrix is not the average distortion (a nonlinearity,
not an error). Unlike the log-det, additivity Information = Sample + Correlation does NOT hold: the three
rows are independent magnitudes. Unlike the affine distance, the Frobenius norm is ALWAYS finite (it needs
no logarithm), so the indefinite numeric-Fisher cells still get a (large) value; they are marked with an x
(non positive-definite central matrix) rather than greyed. All from 433ed13 (numeric F), noise 0.1.
-->

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
library(tidyverse); library(data.table); library(patchwork)
```

```{r params}
DATA_DIR <- "../data/433ed13"; NSIM <- 10000; NOISE <- "0.1"
ALGOS    <- c("macro_NR", "macro_NMR", "macro_R", "macro_MR", "macro_IR")   # established order (matches every other figure)
ALGO_LAB <- c(macro_NR="NR", macro_NMR="MNR", macro_R="R", macro_MR="MR", macro_IR="IR")
alg_f    <- function(a) factor(ALGO_LAB[a], levels = unname(ALGO_LAB[ALGOS]))

# --- bias block: SCORE-BASED bias = inv(median Gaussian FIM) . mean-score, at the truth (battery_sim).
# Defined even where the MLE does not converge -> more cells than the empirical theta_pool - theta_sim. ---
BPAR   <- c(off = 1, Num_ch_mean = 5)
BP_LAB <- c(off = "bias~log[10]~k[off]", Num_ch_mean = "bias~log[10]~N[ch]")
SC  <- "Probit_statistics_Moment_statistics_Sum_dlogL"                        # summed score at the truth
FIM <- "Probit_statistics_Moment_statistics_Sum_Gaussian_Fisher_Information"  # Gaussian FIM at the truth
CIW_MAX <- 2.0   # a bias cell is invalid (x) if inv(F).score 95% CI spans more than this (dex)
# the established RdBu ramp used by every distortion figure
RAMP <- c("#021024","#053061","#2166AC","#4393C3","#92C5DE","#D1E5F0","#F7F7F7",
          "#FDDBC7","#F4A582","#D6604D","#B2182B","#67001F","#3d0011")
# PERCEPTUALLY-uniform diverging steps (interpolated in Lab): each band is one equal-perceptual step from the
# white centre band. For a ONE-SIDED magnitude (first edge = 0) findInterval(0, breaks) = 1 -> no blue side,
# all bands from the white [0, e1] band outward are red (white = self-consistent, darkest = maximally distorted).
bandcols <- function(breaks) {
  n   <- length(breaks) - 1
  ctr <- findInterval(0, breaks)                 # index of the white centre band
  nb  <- ctr - 1; nr <- n - ctr; K <- max(nb, nr, 1)
  blue <- colorRampPalette(rev(RAMP[1:7]), space = "Lab")(K + 1)   # white -> vivid -> darkest blue, equal steps
  red  <- colorRampPalette(RAMP[7:13],     space = "Lab")(K + 1)   # white -> vivid -> darkest red
  cols <- rep(RAMP[7], n)
  if (nb > 0) cols[seq(ctr - 1, 1)] <- blue[2:(nb + 1)]   # band nearest white = step 1
  if (nr > 0) cols[seq(ctr + 1, n)] <- red[2:(nr + 1)]
  cols
}
# eLife number format: superscript 10^n for >=1000, infinity symbol for Inf; parse()-able strings.
fmt_big <- function(x) ifelse(is.infinite(x), "infinity",
             ifelse(x >= 1000, paste0("10^", round(log10(x))), formatC(x, format = "g", digits = 3)))

# bias: LINEAR factor (estimate/truth) edges, round, symmetric, white centred at 1; breaks in log10.
B_LIN    <- c(0.2, 0.33, 0.5, 0.67, 0.8, 0.87, 0.9, 0.95, 0.99, 1.01, 1.05, 1.1, 1.15, 1.25, 1.5, 2, 3, 5)  # fine near 1 (IR)
B_BREAKS <- log10(B_LIN)
B_PAL    <- bandcols(B_BREAKS)
B_LAB    <- fmt_big(B_LIN)

# --- Frobenius-norm decomposition block ---
MATS    <- c(Information = "Probit_statistics_Likelihood_Information_Distortion",
             Sample      = "Probit_statistics_Likelihood_Sample_Distortion",
             Correlation = "Probit_statistics_Likelihood_Correlation_Distortion")
MAT_LAB <- c(Information = "Frobenius~information", Sample = "Frobenius~sample", Correlation = "Frobenius~correlation")
# Frobenius norm is a linear MAGNITUDE (0 = self-consistent); plotted directly. Fine near 0, coarsening
# outward; huge dynamic range (IR/R ~0.1-0.7 to NR/MNR ~10^2-10^5), so the outer bands are decades.
# Labels = the edge value (superscript 10^n for >=1000). up to 10^4 then Inf.
FROB_LIN    <- c(0, 0.1, 0.2, 0.3, 0.5, 0.7, 1, 1.5, 2, 3, 5, 10, 30, 100, 300, 1e3, 1e4, Inf)
FROB_BREAKS <- FROB_LIN
FROB_PAL    <- bandcols(FROB_BREAKS)
FROB_LABV   <- fmt_big(FROB_LIN)
```

```{r load-bias}
read_sim <- function(a) rbindlist(lapply(Sys.glob(file.path(DATA_DIR,
  sprintf("figure_3_nch_*_nsim_%d_%s_noise_%s_battery_sim.csv", NSIM, a, NOISE))), function(p) {
    d <- fread(p, skip = 1)
    d[statistic == "mean" & (
        (component_path == FIM & probit == "quantile" & quantile_level == 0.5) |
        (component_path == SC  & (probit == "mean" |
           (probit == "quantile" & quantile_level %in% c(0.025, 0.975))))),
      .(component_path, probit, quantile_level, Num_ch, interval_in_tau, param_index, param_col, value)]
  }))[, algo := a]
sraw <- rbindlist(lapply(ALGOS, read_sim))[Num_ch >= 10]
sraw[, ck := paste(algo, Num_ch, interval_in_tau)]
sp <- split(sraw[component_path == SC],  by = "ck")
fp <- split(sraw[component_path == FIM], by = "ck")
oc <- function(ck) {
  fk <- fp[[ck]]; sk <- sp[[ck]]
  if (is.null(fk) || is.null(sk) || nrow(fk) != 36) return(NULL)
  Fm <- matrix(0, 6, 6); Fm[cbind(fk$param_index + 1, fk$param_col + 1)] <- fk$value; Fm <- (Fm + t(Fm)) / 2
  sing <- FALSE
  Fi <- tryCatch(solve(Fm), error = function(e) { sing <<- TRUE; MASS::ginv(Fm) })
  gv <- function(cond) { r <- sk[cond][order(param_index), value]; if (length(r) == 6) r else rep(NA_real_, 6) }
  pt <- as.numeric(Fi %*% gv(sk$probit == "mean"))
  lo <- as.numeric(Fi %*% gv(sk$probit == "quantile" & sk$quantile_level == 0.025))
  hi <- as.numeric(Fi %*% gv(sk$probit == "quantile" & sk$quantile_level == 0.975))
  o <- fk[1]
  data.table(algo = o$algo, Num_ch = o$Num_ch, interval_in_tau = o$interval_in_tau,
             off = pt[2], off_ok = is.finite(pt[2]) & !sing & abs(hi[2] - lo[2]) < CIW_MAX,
             nch = pt[6], nch_ok = is.finite(pt[6]) & !sing & abs(hi[6] - lo[6]) < CIW_MAX)
}
bias <- rbindlist(lapply(names(fp), oc)) %>% { rbind(
    .[, .(algo, Num_ch, interval_in_tau, pn = "off",         val = off, valid = off_ok)],
    .[, .(algo, Num_ch, interval_in_tau, pn = "Num_ch_mean", val = nch, valid = nch_ok)]) } %>%
  as_tibble() %>% filter(is.finite(val)) %>%
  mutate(row = factor(BP_LAB[pn], levels = unname(BP_LAB[names(BPAR)])),
         algo_f = alg_f(algo), lx = log10(interval_in_tau), ly = log10(Num_ch))
```

```{r load-decomp}
# per-cell contour cell width in log space (for the grey missing-data-cell tiles)
cellw <- function(v) { u <- sort(unique(v))
  e <- c(u[1] - diff(u[1:2]) / 2, (head(u, -1) + tail(u, -1)) / 2,
         u[length(u)] + diff(u[(length(u) - 1):length(u)]) / 2)
  setNames(diff(e), as.character(u))[as.character(v)] }
# read the full central (probit = mean) 6x6 distortion matrices for Information/Sample/Correlation
read_mat <- function(a) rbindlist(lapply(Sys.glob(file.path(DATA_DIR,
  sprintf("figure_3_nch_*_nsim_%d_%s_noise_%s_battery_pool.csv", NSIM, a, NOISE))), function(p) {
    d <- fread(p, skip = 1)
    d[component_path %in% MATS & probit == "mean" & statistic == "value" &
        value_row <= 5 & value_col <= 5,
      .(component_path, Num_ch, interval_in_tau, value_row, value_col, value)]
  }))[, algo := a]
mraw <- rbindlist(lapply(ALGOS, read_mat))[Num_ch >= 10]
# Frobenius norm ||D - I||_F of each central matrix (always finite); flag cells whose central matrix is not
# positive-definite (an indefinite numeric-Fisher cell) for an x marker.
dist <- mraw[, {
    M <- matrix(NA_real_, 6, 6); M[cbind(value_row + 1, value_col + 1)] <- value; M <- (M + t(M)) / 2
    if (anyNA(M)) { list(m = NA_real_, indef = TRUE) } else {
      ev <- eigen(M, symmetric = TRUE, only.values = TRUE)$values
      list(m = sqrt(sum((M - diag(6))^2)), indef = any(ev <= 0)) }
  }, by = .(algo, component_path, Num_ch, interval_in_tau)]
dist <- as_tibble(dist) %>%
  mutate(q = names(MATS)[match(component_path, MATS)],
         row = factor(MAT_LAB[q], levels = unname(MAT_LAB[names(MATS)])),
         algo_f = alg_f(algo), lx = log10(interval_in_tau), ly = log10(Num_ch),
         defined = is.finite(m)) %>%
  filter(Num_ch >= 10) %>%
  group_by(algo, q) %>% mutate(w = cellw(lx), h = cellw(ly)) %>% ungroup()
```

```{r plot, fig.width = 7.2, fig.height = 9.2}
X_BRK <- log10(c(.01,.1,1)); X_LAB <- c(".01",".1","1")
Y_BRK <- log10(c(10,100,1000,10000)); Y_LAB <- c("10","100","1e3","1e4")
base_layers <- function(xlab) list(
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = X_BRK, labels = X_LAB),
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = Y_BRK, labels = Y_LAB),
  labs(x = xlab, y = expression(N[ch])),
  theme_bw(base_size = 9, base_family = "Helvetica"),
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), panel.spacing = unit(0.28, "cm"), legend.position = "none",
        axis.text = element_text(size = 6.8), axis.title = element_text(size = 8),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 7.3), plot.tag = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold")))
dot   <- function(d) geom_point(data = d, aes(lx, ly), size = .3, colour = "grey15", inherit.aes = FALSE)
cross <- function(d) geom_point(data = d, aes(lx, ly), shape = 4, size = .8, stroke = .35,
                                colour = "grey30", inherit.aes = FALSE)
clamp <- function(v, b) pmin(pmax(v, min(b)), max(b))

gB <- ggplot(filter(bias, valid), aes(lx, ly, z = clamp(val, B_BREAKS))) +
  geom_contour_filled(breaks = B_BREAKS) + scale_fill_manual(values = B_PAL, drop = FALSE, guide = "none") +
  geom_contour(breaks = c(log10(1/1.15), log10(1.15)), colour = "grey30", linewidth = .3, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_contour(breaks = c(log10(1/1.05), log10(1.05)), colour = "black",  linewidth = .35) +  # IR's own p99 precision
  dot(filter(bias, valid)) + cross(filter(bias, !valid)) +
  facet_grid(row ~ algo_f, labeller = labeller(row = label_parsed)) +
  base_layers(expression(Delta %.% k[off])) + labs(tag = "A")

# panel B: Frobenius norm, one-sided sequential; indefinite central matrices marked with x (still coloured).
gF <- ggplot(filter(dist, defined), aes(lx, ly, z = clamp(m, FROB_BREAKS))) +
  geom_contour_filled(breaks = FROB_BREAKS) + scale_fill_manual(values = FROB_PAL, drop = FALSE, guide = "none") +
  geom_contour(breaks = 0.5, colour = "grey30", linewidth = .3, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_contour(breaks = 0.2, colour = "black",  linewidth = .35) +   # self-consistency 0.2 (solid) / 0.5 (dashed)
  geom_tile(data = filter(dist, !defined), aes(lx, ly, width = w, height = h),
            fill = "grey85", inherit.aes = FALSE) +                  # missing matrix data
  dot(filter(dist, defined & !indef)) + cross(filter(dist, defined & indef)) +
  facet_grid(row ~ algo_f, labeller = labeller(row = label_parsed)) +
  base_layers(expression(Delta %.% k[off])) + labs(tag = "B")

# horizontal stepped colour key: numbers sit AT the boundaries between colours (linear magnitudes).
# the self-consistency contour values (solid, dashed) are marked ON the bar in their line style.
colorbar <- function(pal, edge_lab, title, lin, sol = numeric(0), dsh = numeric(0)) {
  n <- length(pal); pos <- function(v) match(v, lin) - 0.5
  marks <- c(
    if (length(dsh)) list(annotate("segment", x = pos(dsh), xend = pos(dsh), y = 0.5, yend = 2.0,
                                   colour = "grey25", linewidth = .35, linetype = "dashed")),
    if (length(sol)) list(annotate("segment", x = pos(sol), xend = pos(sol), y = 0.5, yend = 2.0,
                                   colour = "black", linewidth = .5)))
  ggplot(data.frame(x = seq_len(n)), aes(x, 1, fill = factor(x))) +
    geom_tile(colour = "grey92", linewidth = .15) + marks +
    scale_fill_manual(values = pal, guide = "none") +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0.5, n + 0.5, 1), labels = parse(text = edge_lab), expand = c(0, 0),
                       position = "bottom") +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0.5, 2.0), clip = "off") +
    labs(y = title) + theme_void(base_family = "Helvetica") +
    theme(axis.title.y = element_text(angle = 0, size = 7.5, hjust = 1, vjust = .5, margin = margin(r = 4)),
          axis.text.x = element_text(size = 6), axis.ticks.x = element_line(colour = "grey55", linewidth = .3),
          axis.ticks.length.x = unit(2, "pt"), plot.margin = margin(5, 22, 3, 2))
}
cbB <- colorbar(B_PAL,    B_LAB,     "bias factor",              B_LIN,    sol = c(0.95, 1.05), dsh = c(0.87, 1.15))
cbF <- colorbar(FROB_PAL, FROB_LABV, "Frobenius norm", FROB_LIN, sol = 0.2, dsh = 0.5)

g <- gB / cbB / gF / cbF + plot_layout(heights = c(2, 0.16, 3, 0.16))
g
ggsave("Figure_5_master_frobenius.pdf", g, width = 7.2, height = 9.2)
```

```{r ranges-note}
dist %>% group_by(algo, q) %>%
  summarise(median_frobenius = round(median(m, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
            n_indefinite = sum(indef, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = q, values_from = c(median_frobenius, n_indefinite)) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>% print()
```
